sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **PayPal connector** integrates Shielva with the paypal platform via its public API. It is implemented as `PayPalConnector`, a subclass of `BaseConnector`, and is responsible for two complementary capabilities: **ingesting data** from PayPal into the Shielva knowledge base as `NormalizedDocument` records, and **exposing direct API methods** that downstream Shielva workflows can call to read or mutate live PayPal resources.

Connects to PayPal REST API v2 to sync transactions, orders, and payments into the Shielva knowledge base using OAuth2 client credentials authentication.

### Business problem solved

Teams using PayPal typically have valuable data trapped behind an API: customer records, conversations, configuration, analytics, files. Wiring each of those into a search index, an LLM context window, or an automation by hand requires writing the same authentication, pagination, retry, and normalization code repeatedly. This connector packages all of that — authentication (OAUTH2), paginated sync, error mapping, exception hierarchy, document normalization — behind a small, stable interface so application code never touches the underlying REST endpoints directly.

### Connector identity

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Provider | `paypal` |
| Service | `paypal` |
| Connector class | `PayPalConnector` |
| Connector type | `paypal` |
| Auth type | `oauth2` |
| Version | `1.0.0` |
| SDK package | `requests` |

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
Get the PayPal connector running in under 5 minutes.

### 1. Create a PayPal REST App

1. Log in to the [PayPal Developer Dashboard](https://developer.paypal.com/developer/applications/)
2. Click **Create App** under **My Apps & Credentials**
3. Select **Merchant** as the app type
4. Click **Create App**
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** from the app detail page

> **Tip:** The dashboard shows both Sandbox and Live credentials on the same page. Use Sandbox credentials during development.

### 2. Install the Connector

In the Shielva ACP:
1. Navigate to **Integrations → PayPal**
2. Click **Connect**
3. Enter your **Client ID** and **Client Secret**
4. Optionally set **Sandbox Mode** to `true` for testing
5. Click **Install**

The connector will verify credentials via `POST https://api-m.paypal.com/v1/oauth2/token` and set status to `ONLINE` on success.

### 3. Verify

```python
result = await connector.health_check()
assert result.health == ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY
```

### 4. Run Your First Sync

```python
# Full sync — fetches last 31 days of transactions
result = await connector.sync(full=True)
print(f"Synced {result.documents_synced} transactions")
```

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
The PayPal connector uses **OAuth2 client credentials** flow — the industry-standard machine-to-machine authentication pattern.

### Credentials

| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Client ID | `client_id` | text | Yes | PayPal REST app Client ID |
| Client Secret | `client_secret` | password | Yes | PayPal REST app Client Secret |
| Sandbox Mode | `sandbox` | string | No | Set `true` for sandbox, empty for live |

### How it works

1. The connector sends a POST to `/v1/oauth2/token` with HTTP Basic Auth (client_id:client_secret)
2. PayPal returns an `access_token` valid for `expires_in` seconds (typically 32400 = 9 hours)
3. The connector caches the token and injects `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on every API call
4. The token is refreshed automatically 60 seconds before expiry to avoid mid-request failures

```
POST /v1/oauth2/token
Authorization: Basic base64(client_id:client_secret)
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials
```

### Sandbox vs Live

| Mode | Base URL | When to use |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Sandbox | `https://api-m.sandbox.paypal.com` | Development & testing |
| Live | `https://api-m.paypal.com` | Production |

### Security

- The Client Secret is stored encrypted in the Shielva vault and is never logged
- OAuth2 tokens are held in process memory only; not persisted to disk
- Token refresh is fully automatic — no manual intervention needed

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
Every `install_field` listed below is collected from the user **once** at install time and stored in the connector's encrypted config. `config_keys` are values the connector derives at runtime (e.g. tokens) and persists for subsequent calls.

### Install fields

| Key | Label | Type | Required | Description |
|-----|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `client_id` | Client ID | `text` | ✅ | Your PayPal REST app Client ID. Found in the PayPal Developer Dashboard under My Apps & Credentials. |
| `client_secret` | Client Secret | `password` | ✅ | Your PayPal REST app Client Secret. Found in the PayPal Developer Dashboard next to the Client ID. |
| `sandbox` | Sandbox Mode | `string` | — | Set to 'true' to use the PayPal sandbox environment for testing. Leave empty or set to 'false' for live/production. |

### Runtime config keys

_No runtime-derived config keys for this connector._

### Defaults & validation

- All values are validated on `initialize()` — missing required fields raise `MissingConfigError`, malformed values raise `InvalidConfigError`.
- Numeric and boolean fields are coerced from strings (e.g. `"true"` → `True`) so configuration loaded from environment variables is accepted unchanged.
- Secrets are never echoed back in responses or logs; only field keys appear in audit entries.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes 7 API methods across 5 resource domains.

children.title: Install / Authenticate
children.content:
Validate credentials by acquiring an OAuth2 token.

### `install()`

```python
result = await connector.install()
# InstallResult(
#   health=ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY,
#   auth_status=AuthStatus.CONNECTED,
#   connector_id="paypal_live",
#   message="Connected to PayPal (live mode)"
# )
```

Returns `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` if either `client_id` or `client_secret` is empty.
Returns `INVALID_CREDENTIALS` if PayPal rejects the credentials with a 401.
Returns `FAILED` for other errors.

children.title: Health Check
children.content:
Verify credentials are still valid by re-acquiring an OAuth2 token.

### `health_check()`

```python
result = await connector.health_check()
# HealthCheckResult(
#   health=ConnectorHealth.HEALTHY,
#   auth_status=AuthStatus.CONNECTED,
#   message="PayPal API is reachable"
# )
```

Returns `DEGRADED` on transient network errors.
Returns `OFFLINE` when the circuit breaker has opened (after 5 consecutive failures).

children.title: Sync
children.content:
Sync PayPal transactions into the Shielva knowledge base.

### `sync(full=False, since=None, kb_id="")`

```python
# Full sync — last 31 days
result = await connector.sync(full=True, kb_id="kb_paypal")

# Incremental sync since a specific date
from datetime import datetime, timezone
since = datetime(2024, 6, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
result = await connector.sync(since=since, kb_id="kb_paypal")

print(result.documents_found)   # total transactions found
print(result.documents_synced)  # successfully normalized + ingested
print(result.documents_failed)  # normalization failures (partial sync)
```

**How it works:**
1. Computes `start_date` / `end_date` from `full` flag or `since` datetime
2. Calls `GET /v1/reporting/transactions` with page-based pagination
3. Normalizes each transaction into a `ConnectorDocument` with stable SHA-256 source_id
4. Ingests into the knowledge base when `kb_id` is provided

**PayPal endpoint:** `GET /v1/reporting/transactions`

children.title: Transactions
children.content:
Retrieve transaction history from PayPal Reporting.

### `list_transactions(start_date, end_date, page=1, page_size=100)`

Returns a paginated list of transactions within a date range.

```python
txns = await connector.list_transactions(
    start_date="2024-01-01T00:00:00Z",
    end_date="2024-01-31T23:59:59Z",
    page=1,
    page_size=100,
)
# Returns:
# {
#   "transaction_details": [
#     {
#       "transaction_info": {
#         "transaction_id": "8B916932BT579310G",
#         "transaction_amount": {"currency_code": "USD", "value": "14.75"},
#         "transaction_status": "S",
#         ...
#       },
#       "payer_info": {"email_address": "buyer@example.com", ...}
#     }
#   ],
#   "total_pages": 3,
#   "total_items": 250
# }
```

**Date format:** ISO 8601 with seconds + Z (e.g. `2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`)

**PayPal endpoint:** `GET /v1/reporting/transactions`

> **Note:** PayPal's Transaction Search API requires the **Transaction Search** feature to be enabled in your PayPal Developer app settings.

children.title: Orders
children.content:
Retrieve PayPal orders created via the v2 Checkout API.

### `get_order(order_id)`

Fetch a single order by its ID.

```python
order = await connector.get_order("5O190127TN364715T")
# Returns:
# {
#   "id": "5O190127TN364715T",
#   "status": "COMPLETED",
#   "create_time": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
#   "purchase_units": [
#     {"amount": {"currency_code": "USD", "value": "14.75"}}
#   ],
#   "payer": {"email_address": "buyer@example.com", "name": {...}}
# }
```

**PayPal endpoint:** `GET /v2/checkout/orders/{order_id}`

**Order statuses:** `CREATED`, `SAVED`, `APPROVED`, `VOIDED`, `COMPLETED`, `PAYER_ACTION_REQUIRED`

children.title: Payments
children.content:
Retrieve PayPal payment records.

### `list_payments(page_size=20, page=1)`

Returns a paginated list of PayPal payment records.

```python
payments = await connector.list_payments(page_size=20, page=1)
# Returns:
# {
#   "payments": [
#     {"id": "PAY-...", "state": "approved", "transactions": [...]}
#   ],
#   "count": 20,
#   "next_id": "PAY-...next..."
# }
```

**PayPal endpoint:** `GET /v1/payments/payment`

children.title: Balance
children.content:
Query the current account balance across all currencies.

### `get_balance()`

```python
balance = await connector.get_balance()
# Returns:
# {
#   "balances": [
#     {
#       "currency": "USD",
#       "primary": true,
#       "total_balance": {"currency_code": "USD", "value": "1250.00"},
#       "available_balance": {"currency_code": "USD", "value": "1250.00"},
#       "withheld_balance": {"currency_code": "USD", "value": "0.00"}
#     }
#   ]
# }
```

**PayPal endpoint:** `GET /v1/reporting/balances`

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content:
Every record this connector emits into the Shielva knowledge base is a `ConnectorDocument` — a normalized envelope with a stable `source_id`, a resource type discriminator, and a payload of provider-specific fields. The shape per resource is documented below; the contract is enforced by the `normalize_*` helpers in `helpers/utils.py` (or `helpers/normalizer.py`).

### Envelope fields (shared)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `source_id` | `str` | SHA-256-derived 16-char ID, stable across resyncs |
| `resource_type` | `str` | Discriminator: `message`, `contact`, `event`, etc. |
| `payload` | `dict` | Provider-native fields, untouched JSON-safe values |
| `created_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `updated_at` | `str` | RFC 3339 timestamp from the provider |
| `tenant_id` | `str` | Multi-tenant scope — never `None` for stored records |

### Resource models

#### `InstallResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `health` | `ConnectorHealth` |
| `auth_status` | `AuthStatus` |
| `connector_id` | `str` |
| `message` | `str` |

#### `HealthCheckResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `health` | `ConnectorHealth` |
| `auth_status` | `AuthStatus` |
| `message` | `str` |

#### `SyncResult`

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `status` | `SyncStatus` |
| `documents_found` | `int` |
| `documents_synced` | `int` |
| `documents_failed` | `int` |
| `message` | `str` |

#### `ConnectorDocument`

Normalized document emitted by the connector into the knowledge base.

| Field | Type |
|-------|------|
| `source_id` | `str` |
| `title` | `str` |
| `content` | `str` |
| `connector_id` | `str` |
| `tenant_id` | `str` |
| `source_url` | `str` |
| `metadata` | `dict[str, Any]` |


### `source_id` determinism

All resource IDs are computed by SHA-256-hashing the provider's stable identifier (e.g. message ID, contact ID, event UUID) plus the resource type, then truncating to 16 characters. This guarantees that re-syncing the same resource produces the same Shielva document ID — enabling idempotent ingestion and incremental updates without duplicates.

sections.title: Sync Behavior
sections.content:
`sync()` is the bulk ingestion entry point. A single call pulls every resource this connector knows how to sync from PayPal, normalizes each one into a `ConnectorDocument`, and returns a `SyncResult` summarizing what was processed.

### Sync lifecycle

1. **Listing** — the connector requests the first page of each resource type from the provider's list endpoint.
2. **Pagination** — subsequent pages are fetched until the provider signals end-of-stream (cursor exhaustion, `has_more=false`, or empty page).
3. **Normalization** — each raw record is passed through the resource-specific `normalize_*` helper, which extracts fields, computes the `source_id`, and emits a `ConnectorDocument`.
4. **Deduplication** — when the same resource appears across pages (rare, but possible during eventual-consistency windows), the deterministic `source_id` ensures the downstream knowledge base treats it as a single record, not a duplicate.
5. **Partial-failure tolerance** — a per-resource failure is logged and the sync continues with the next resource. The returned `SyncResult` exposes `status=PARTIAL` and an `errors` list when this happens.

### Pagination strategy

- **Cursor-based** providers (most modern APIs) — the connector tracks the `next_cursor` field on each page and stops when the provider omits it.
- **Offset/limit** providers — the connector requests fixed-size pages and stops when a page returns fewer records than `limit`.
- **Token-based (e.g. `next_page_token`)** — same as cursor-based but the token is opaque and forwarded verbatim.

### Rate-limit handling

When the provider returns `429 Too Many Requests`, the connector honors the `Retry-After` header if present, otherwise applies exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s) with full jitter. The retry budget per request is bounded so a misbehaving provider cannot stall the sync indefinitely.

### Incremental sync

The connector treats every `sync()` as a full re-walk of the listing endpoints. Idempotency is achieved via stable `source_id`s rather than incremental cursors. If the provider exposes a `since` / `updated_after` parameter, the connector uses the last successful sync timestamp; otherwise it pulls the full list and lets deduplication handle overlap.

### Features enabled

This connector is configured with: circuit_breaker, error_handling, normalizer, oauth2, pagination, rate_limiting, retry, sandbox_support.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Every failure mode this connector encounters maps to a typed exception. Catching the base class — `PayPalError` — handles every connector-raised error in one block; catching a specific subclass lets you react to a particular failure mode.

### Exception hierarchy

```
PayPalError(Exception)
  PayPalAuthError(PayPalError)
  PayPalInvalidCredentialsError(PayPalAuthError)
  PayPalRateLimitError(PayPalError)
  PayPalNotFoundError(PayPalError)
  PayPalNetworkError(PayPalError)
  PayPalServerError(PayPalError)
  PayPalTokenError(PayPalAuthError)
```

### When each is raised

- **`PayPalError`** — Base exception for all PayPal connector errors.
- **`PayPalAuthError`** — Raised when PayPal rejects the client credentials (401/403).
- **`PayPalInvalidCredentialsError`** — Raised when the client_id or client_secret is invalid or explicitly rejected.
- **`PayPalRateLimitError`** — Raised on 429 Too Many Requests from PayPal.
- **`PayPalNotFoundError`** — Raised when a requested PayPal resource does not exist (404).
- **`PayPalNetworkError`** — Raised on transient network failures (timeouts, connection errors).
- **`PayPalServerError`** — Raised on 5xx responses from PayPal.
- **`PayPalTokenError`** — Raised when token acquisition fails.

### Retry policy

- **Transient HTTP errors** (network resets, `502`, `503`, `504`) — retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff + jitter.
- **Rate-limit responses** (`429`) — retry honoring `Retry-After`, capped at 5 attempts.
- **Authentication errors** (`401`, `403`) — not retried; surface immediately so the caller can re-authorize.
- **4xx client errors** (`400`, `404`, `422`) — not retried; raised as a typed exception so the caller can fix the request.

### Circuit breaker

A per-connector circuit breaker opens after 5 consecutive failures within a 30-second window and stays open for 30 seconds. While open, every method call raises `CircuitBreakerOpen` immediately instead of incurring the network round-trip — protecting the provider from a thundering-herd retry storm when it is genuinely down.

sections.title: Testing
sections.content:
The connector ships with a comprehensive test suite.

### Unit Tests (54 tests, zero I/O)

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/paypal_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
# Expected: 54 passed in < 15 seconds
```

All HTTP calls are mocked via `AsyncMock` — no PayPal account required.

**Coverage:**
- `install()` — success (live + sandbox), missing credentials (3 cases), invalid, auth error, exception
- `health_check()` — success, missing, invalid, network error, general exception
- `sync()` — empty, with data, pagination (2 pages), API error → FAILED, partial failure
- `list_transactions()` — basic + pagination params
- `get_order()` — success, not found (raises exception)
- `list_payments()` — basic + custom page params
- `get_balance()` — success, empty response
- Sandbox flag parsing (3 cases)
- `normalize_transaction()` — all fields, stable ID, payer info, sandbox URL, live URL, content fields
- `normalize_order()` — all fields, stable ID, payer info, purchase units, description, URLs, empty payer
- CircuitBreaker state machine — starts closed, opens at threshold, below threshold, closes on success, custom threshold
- Connector lifecycle — aclose noop, aclose + http_client cleanup, context manager
- Config extraction + CONNECTOR_TYPE/AUTH_TYPE constants
- `_ensure_client()` on-demand creation

### Running Against Sandbox (optional)

Set real PayPal sandbox credentials and run a smoke test:

```python
import asyncio
from connector import PayPalConnector

async def smoke_test():
    connector = PayPalConnector(config={
        "client_id": "YOUR_SANDBOX_CLIENT_ID",
        "client_secret": "YOUR_SANDBOX_CLIENT_SECRET",
        "sandbox": "true",
    })
    result = await connector.install()
    print(result)

asyncio.run(smoke_test())
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
Common issues and their resolutions.

### `auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS` after install

**Cause:** Wrong Client ID or Client Secret, or sandbox/live credentials mixed up.

**Fix:** Go to the [PayPal Developer Dashboard](https://developer.paypal.com/developer/applications/), open your app, and copy the correct credentials. If you're testing, use Sandbox credentials and set `sandbox=true`.

---

### `list_transactions` returns empty `transaction_details`

**Cause:** No transactions in the specified date range, or Transaction Search is disabled.

**Fix:**
1. Verify the date range contains real transactions in the PayPal account.
2. In PayPal Developer Dashboard, open your app and enable **Transaction Search** under Features.
3. For sandbox, create some test transactions first via the Sandbox dashboard.

---

### `PayPalNetworkError: Request timed out`

**Cause:** Transient network issue or PayPal API slowness.

**Fix:** The connector retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff. Check [PayPal System Status](https://www.paypalobjects.com) for ongoing incidents. If persistent, contact PayPal support.

---

### Circuit breaker opens (health=OFFLINE after many failures)

**Cause:** 5 consecutive failures triggered the circuit breaker.

**Fix:** Wait 60 seconds for the circuit to enter HALF_OPEN. Run `health_check()` to probe. Fix the underlying issue (network outage, wrong credentials) before the circuit auto-resets.

---

### `get_order` returns 404

**Cause:** Order ID doesn't exist, or you're querying live orders with sandbox credentials (or vice versa).

**Fix:** Ensure the `sandbox` setting matches the environment where the order was created. Sandbox orders cannot be retrieved from the live environment.

---

### Token expires mid-operation

**Cause:** Should not happen with this connector — tokens are refreshed 60 seconds before expiry.

**Fix:** If it does occur (e.g. system clock drift), the next API call will automatically acquire a fresh token. No action needed.

sections.title: FAQ
sections.content:
### Does this connector support multiple accounts of the same provider?

Yes. Each PayPal account is a separate connector *instance* with its own `connector_id`, install fields, and encrypted credentials. The provider key in the catalog is shared, but the multi-tenant data layer keeps the instances strictly isolated.

### Can I sync only some resource types?

Yes. The connector exposes a `sync_resources` config option (a list of resource type strings). When set, `sync()` walks only the listed types and skips the rest. Leave it unset to sync everything the connector knows about.

### How does deletion propagate?

On each `sync()`, the connector compares the set of `source_id`s returned by PayPal against the set it last persisted to the knowledge base. IDs that are absent on the new walk are deleted from the knowledge base, so deletions in PayPal propagate to Shielva on the next sync.

### What happens during a rate-limit hit?

The connector inspects `Retry-After` and waits the indicated interval. If the header is absent, it falls back to exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s) with full jitter, retrying up to 5 times. After that it raises a `RateLimitError` so the caller can decide whether to back off further.

### Are credentials encrypted at rest?

Yes — AES-256-GCM with a per-tenant data encryption key derived via HKDF-SHA256 from the master key. Plaintext exists in memory only for the lifetime of an in-flight request; the secret never appears in logs, audit entries, or telemetry.

### How do I add a new method?

1. Add the method to the `PayPalConnector` class in `connector.py`.
2. Add a corresponding `test_<method>` to `tests/test_connector.py` covering happy/empty/error paths.
3. Re-run the documentation generator — the new method will appear in **API Methods** automatically.

### Can the connector run in a worker / serverless context?

Yes. The connector is fully `async` and holds no global state, so it is safe to instantiate per-request (or per-task) inside Celery, AWS Lambda, or a worker pool. For long-running processes, instantiate once and reuse — the underlying HTTP client maintains a keep-alive connection pool.

sections.title: Changelog
sections.content:
All notable changes to the **PayPal connector** are recorded here. This project follows [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/): `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` where `MAJOR` is incremented for breaking interface changes, `MINOR` for backwards-compatible features, and `PATCH` for bug fixes.

## [1.0.0] — Current

### Added
- Initial release of the PayPal connector.
- Full sync of all primary resource types with normalization into `ConnectorDocument`.
- Public connector methods for direct API access (see **API Methods**).
- Typed exception hierarchy with retry-aware classification (see **Error Handling**).
- Health check endpoint for fast credential validation.

### Stability
- Multi-tenant safe — every query is scoped by `tenant_id`.
- Idempotent ingestion — deterministic `source_id` ensures resyncs don't duplicate.
- Circuit breaker protects upstream during sustained outage.

### Upgrade notes
- This is the initial release; no upgrade path is required.
- Future PATCH releases are guaranteed not to break existing config or method signatures.
- MINOR releases may introduce new optional methods or features; existing call sites continue to work unchanged.
- MAJOR releases will be accompanied by a migration guide in this section.
